mission.title: strimzi: Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization
mission.description: Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization. Requested by 32+ users.

steps.title: Check current strimzi deployment
steps.description:
Verify your strimzi version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n strimzi -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi
helm list -n strimzi 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working strimzi installation.

steps.title: Review strimzi configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant strimzi configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n strimzi -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi
kubectl get configmap -n strimzi -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=strimzi
```
Hi! We are looking at using Strimzi to deploy Kafka Connect clusters using Kafka brokers deployed using Amazon MSK.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization
steps.description:
Update the configuration generator scripts to support arbitrary configuration properties. The `KAFKA_CONNECT_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG` environment variable is open ended and non-opinionated. For this reason, it can be used to pass in additional configuration properties. This allows for support of
```yaml
ssl.truststore.location=<PATH_TO_TRUST_STORE_FILE>
security.protocol=SASL_SSL
sasl.mechanism=AWS_MSK_IAM
sasl.jaas.config=software.amazon.msk.auth.iam.IAMLoginModule required;
sasl.client.callback.handler.class=software.amazon.msk.auth.iam.IAMClientCallbackHandler
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n strimzi -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi
kubectl get events -n strimzi --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for AWS MSK IAM authorization" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Update the configuration generator scripts to support arbitrary configuration properties. The `KAFKA_CONNECT_ADDITIONAL_CONFIG` environment variable is open ended and non-opinionated. For this reason, it can be used to pass in additional configuration properties. This allows for support of arbitrary authentications mechanisms that were not possible before.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with strimzi installed or the issue environment reproducible.
